edges.description: Failure to assemble Complex IV yields a catalytically deficient holoenzyme.
edges.source: COX5A Structural Subunit Loss and Failed Complex IV Assembly
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Loss of oxidative ATP synthesis forces anaerobic glycolysis and lactate accumulation.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: The bioenergetic deficit injures high-demand tissues, including the pulmonary vasculature.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Lactate overproduction manifests clinically as lactic acidemia.
Pulmonary vascular involvement manifests as early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension.
Systemic energy failure manifests as failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Biallelic COX5A variants disrupt a nuclear-encoded structural subunit of Complex IV. The reported variant lies within the conserved COX5A/COX4 interface domain, altering the interaction between these subunits during holoenzyme biogenesis. As a result the monomeric COX1 assembly intermediate accumulates and the mature enzyme fails to assemble, yielding isolated Complex IV biogenesis failure.
nodes.id: COX5A Structural Subunit Loss and Failed Complex IV Assembly

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: COX5A is a nuclear-encoded subunit of mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: the variant lies within the evolutionarily conserved COX5A/COX4 interface domain, suggesting that it alters the interaction between these two subunits during complex IV biogenesis

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: The monomeric COX1 assembly intermediate accumulates demonstrating a function of COX5A in complex IV biogenesis

nodes.description: Failure to thrive accompanied the pulmonary arterial hypertension and lactic acidemia in the affected siblings.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, failure to thrive, and isolated complex IV deficiency

nodes.description: The bioenergetic deficit manifests in high-demand tissues. In COX5A-related disease the dominant organ phenotype is early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension, accompanied by failure to thrive, distinguishing it from the encephalopathy/cardiomyopathy presentations of other nuclear COX deficiencies.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, failure to thrive, and isolated complex IV deficiency

nodes.description: Reduced amounts and activity of assembled Complex IV block electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis. In COX5A-deficient patient fibroblasts the enzymatic activity and protein levels of Complex IV and several of its subunits are reduced.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: the enzymatic activity and protein levels of complex IV and several of its subunits are reduced

nodes.description: With oxidative phosphorylation impaired, pyruvate is shunted to lactate, elevating blood lactate; in COX5A-related disease this presented as lactic acidemia in both affected siblings.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in mitochondrial complex IV subunit COX5A causes pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, and failure to thrive.
evidence.snippet: early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, failure to thrive, and isolated complex IV deficiency

nodes.description: Lactic acidemia (elevated blood lactate), a presenting metabolic feature of COX5A-related Complex IV deficiency.

evidence.snippet: early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension, lactic acidemia, failure to thrive, and isolated complex IV deficiency

nodes.description: Early-onset pulmonary arterial hypertension, the dominant presenting feature of COX5A-related Complex IV deficiency in the two reported siblings.
